title: tetraneuris torreyana
intro: Tetraneuris torreyana, also known as Torrey's yellow paintbrush, is a perennial plant species native to the southwestern United States and northwestern Mexico. It is a member of the sunflower family (Asteraceae) and has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The plant has been used to treat various health issues, such as fever, sore throats, and respiratory problems, due to its expectorant properties, which help to loosen and clear mucus from the body. Herbal preparations of Tetraneuris torreyana include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used to make teas, salves, and other remedies for its medicinal benefits.
